skill.name: sending-emails
skill.description: Guides Mailtrap live email sending integrations using Email API, batch requests, SMTP, SDKs, streams, tokens, and common troubleshooting paths.
skill.summary: Integrate and troubleshoot live Mailtrap email sending through API, SMTP, SDKs, and stream selection.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and network patterns, but review shows they are Markdown documentation examples for Mailtrap Email API, SMTP, and SDK links. No executable skill code, prompt injection, hidden script, or malicious exfiltration path was found; the remaining risk is legitimate live email-sending guidance that can use API tokens and contact external Mailtrap services.

medium_findings.title: Live Email Sending Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill documents real Mailtrap Email API and SMTP sending paths. This is intended behavior, but it can send live email and uses bearer tokens or SMTP credentials if users copy the examples into a configured environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly names live API hosts, SMTP hosts, and token-based authentication. The risk is legitimate email-sending capability rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Curl Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis detected shell command patterns in fenced Markdown examples. The skill file does not execute these commands; they are user-facing examples for Mailtrap API requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content appears inside Markdown code fences and there are no scripts or executable files in the skill. This strongly supports a false positive for automatic command execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Mailtrap URLs Are Documentation Targets
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs, but they point to official Mailtrap documentation, API hosts, SMTP hosts, and SDK repositories. These are expected for an integration guide.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match the stated Mailtrap product and documentation purpose. I found no evidence that the links route data to unrelated or suspicious destinations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Blocker Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static blocker points at front matter description text, not cryptographic code or a hashing algorithm. No evidence found for weak cryptographic behavior in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are YAML description text only. There is no code path, cryptographic API, or algorithm selection at that location.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Blocker Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static blocker points at explanatory Campaigns workflow text. No evidence found for host, user, process, network, or filesystem reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 30 describes Mailtrap Campaigns and UI setup. It does not ask the agent to inspect the local system or collect environment details.

dangerous_patterns.title: Token-Bearing Live Send Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples include Authorization headers and SMTP password guidance using MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN. Users should store tokens safely and avoid pasting real secrets into shared prompts or logs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token references are clear and repeated, but they use environment variable placeholders rather than embedded secrets. The pattern is risky only when users mishandle real credentials.

content.value_statement: Email integrations often fail because teams choose the wrong stream, endpoint, or transport. This skill guides Mailtrap Email API, SMTP, SDK, and batch sending choices with practical troubleshooting context.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Mailtrap platform integrations, official SDKs, HTTP Email API, or SMTP.
Distinguishes transactional, bulk, batch, and campaign sending paths.
Provides Mailtrap API hosts, SMTP hosts, ports, and token header guidance.
Shows request patterns for single-message, bulk, batch, and template-based sends.
Links users to official Mailtrap SDK repositories and documentation pages.
Lists common mistakes that cause wrong-host, wrong-stream, or wrong-credential failures.
limitations: Does not verify domains or configure Mailtrap account settings for the user.
Does not replace official API references for every optional request field.
Does not manage contact lists, Campaigns setup, or deliverability strategy.
Requires users to protect API tokens and confirm current SDK method signatures.

use_cases.title: Wire transactional email in an app
use_cases.description: Choose the API, SDK, or SMTP path for password resets, receipts, notifications, and alerts.

use_cases.title: Fix Mailtrap sending failures
use_cases.description: Check host selection, stream type, token scope, SMTP username, rate limits, and domain verification.

use_cases.title: Plan bulk or batch sending
use_cases.description: Separate promotional bulk streams from batch request mechanics and Campaigns workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a sending path
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right Mailtrap sending method for my app. I need to send [transactional or promotional] email from [platform or language].

prompt_templates.title: Configure SMTP settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mailtrap SMTP configuration checklist for [transactional or bulk] email on [platform]. Include host, port, username, and token handling.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a failed send
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Mailtrap sending problem and suggest likely fixes: [describe error, host, stream, transport, and token scope without sharing secrets].

prompt_templates.title: Design a scalable send flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Mailtrap sending flow for [application]. Compare SDK, HTTP API, batch requests, rate limits, stream choice, retries, and template usage.

output_examples.input: I need to send password reset emails from a Node.js app.
output_examples.output: Use the transactional stream first. Prefer the official Node.js SDK, confirm the current README examples, store the token in an environment variable, and send through the transactional host.

output_examples.input: My promotional email is going through the transactional host.
output_examples.output: Move promotional traffic to the bulk stream. Use the bulk API host or bulk SMTP host, confirm token scope, and avoid treating batch as a separate stream.

output_examples.input: I have 300 different notification emails ready at once.
output_examples.output: Use the batch endpoint on the stream that matches the message content. Keep the request under the documented limit and add retry backoff for rate limits.

best_practices: Prefer platform integrations or official SDKs before direct HTTP or SMTP when they fit the application.
Keep Mailtrap API tokens in environment variables and rotate them when access changes.
Verify the sending domain and stream type before sending live messages.
anti_patterns: Do not send promotional traffic through the transactional host.
Do not paste real Mailtrap tokens into prompts, tickets, source files, or logs.
Do not guess SDK constructors or method names without checking the current README.

faq.question: Does this skill send email by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides integration guidance and examples. Users still control whether commands or application code run.

faq.question: When should I use the Email API instead of SMTP?
faq.answer: Use the Email API or an official SDK for most new integrations. Use SMTP when the platform only supports SMTP.

faq.question: What is the difference between bulk and batch?
faq.answer: Bulk is a promotional sending stream. Batch is a way to submit many messages in one API request.

faq.question: Can this help with Mailtrap templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains template UUID and variable usage, then points to the dedicated template skill for more detail.

faq.question: Does it include complete API schemas?
faq.answer: No. It links to official documentation for full schemas, optional fields, and edge cases.

faq.question: What security precautions matter most?
faq.answer: Protect API tokens, use the correct stream, avoid sharing secrets, and test before sending live email at scale.
